back_translation: All you brothers and sisters, now I want to remind you about the good news that I have proclaimed to you, which you have received, and in this good news you stand firmly.
And by this good news, you also receive salvation, if you hold firmly to the words that I have proclaimed to you. Otherwise, you have believed without result.
Because I delivered to you as the most important matter, according to what I myself also received: that Christ (royally) died for our sins, according to the Scriptures.
And He was (royally) buried, and He was (royally) raised on the third day, according to the Scriptures.
And He (royally) appeared to Cephas, then also to the twelve apostles.
Afterward, He appeared to over five hundred brothers and sisters at one time, the majority of whom are still alive up to the present, but some have already passed away.
Afterward, He appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
Last of all everyone, He appeared also to me, like one who was born prematurely.
Because I am the least one among the apostles, who am not worthy to be called an apostle, because I once persecuted the church of God.

But by the grace of God, I am thus what I am; and the grace of His toward me has not been without result. But I have toiled more than all of them — yet not I myself, but the grace of God which is with me.
Therefore, whether it was I or them, we proclaim like this, and you have believed like this.
But if it is proclaimed that Christ has been raised from the dead, why do some among you say that there is no resurrection from the dead?
But if there is no resurrection from the dead, Christ has not been raised either.
And if Christ has not been raised, our proclamation is without benefit, and your faith is also without benefit.
And we ourselves will appear as false-witnesses against God, because we testified about God that He has raised Christ — whom He did not raise, if in fact the dead are not raised.
Because if the dead are not raised, then Christ also has not been raised.
And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile; you all are still in your sins.
Therefore, those who have passed away in Christ have also perished.
If our hope in Christ is for this life only, then we are people who are most pitiable, more than any other human beings.
But now, Christ has indeed been (royally) raised from the dead — being the firstfruits of those who have passed away.
Because death came through one human being; the resurrection from the dead also comes through one human being.
Because as in Adam all people die, in Christ all people will also be made alive.
But each one according to his own order: Christ is the firstfruits; afterward, those who belong to Christ at His (royal) return.
After that will come the end, when He will (royally) hand over the kingdom to God and Father, when He will (royally) destroy every dominion, every authority, and every power.
Because He must reign as king until He has subdued all enemies under His foot.
The last enemy that will be destroyed is death.

Because "God has (royally) subdued all things under His foot." But when it is said that all things have been subdued, it is clear that this does not include the One who subdued all things under Christ.
And when all things have been subdued under the Son, in that time, the Son Himself will also be under the One who has subdued all things under the Son — so that God may be all things in all things.
If it is not so, then what will those who are baptized for the dead do? If the dead are not raised at all, why do they receive baptism for them?
And why are we ourselves in danger every hour?
I face death every day — I affirm by appealing to the boast that I have in you all, in Jesus Christ our (royal) Lord.
If by human reasoning I have fought wild beasts in the city of Ephesus, what benefit will I receive? If the dead are not raised, "Then let us eat and drink, because tomorrow we will die."
Do not be deceived: "Associating with evil people causes good behavior to deteriorate."
Wake up to right thinking as you ought, and do not continue to sin; because some have no knowledge concerning God. I say this to make you all ashamed.
But there will be someone who asks, "How can the dead be raised up? And with what kind of body will they come?"
Foolish person! What you sow does not become alive unless it dies first.
And what you sow is not the body that will arise; rather, it is just a bare seed — perhaps a wheat seed or some other kind of seed.
But God (royally) gives a body to the seed according to what He (royally) wills, and He (royally) gives a specific body to each kind of seed.
Not all flesh is the same flesh: the flesh of human beings is one type, the flesh of domestic animals is another type, the flesh of birds is another type, and the flesh of fish is another type.
Both bodies in the heavens exist, and bodies on the earth exist; but the splendor of the bodies in the heavens is one type, and the splendor of the bodies on the earth is another type.
The splendor of the sun is one type; the splendor of the moon is another type; and the splendor of the stars is another type — because even from star to star, there is different splendor.
The resurrection from the dead is also like this: what is sown in decayability is raised in non-decayability.
Sown in dishonor, it is raised in splendor. Sown in weakness, it is raised in power.
Sown a body of natural-life, it is raised a body of the spirit. If a body of natural-life exists, a body of the spirit also exists.
According to a written word: "The first Adam became a living spirit-soul." The last Adam is a spirit who gives life.
But the spirit-form did not come first; the natural form came first, and afterward the spirit form.
The first human came from the dust of the earth; the second human came from the heavens.
As the one who came from the dust is, so all those who belong to the dust are; and as the one who came from the heavens is, so all those who belong to the heavens are.
And just as we have worn the (royal) image of the one who is of the dust, we will also wear the (royal) image of the one who is of the heavens.
All you brothers and sisters, I now declare: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; and decayability does not inherit non-decayability.
Behold, I will tell a mystery to you all: we will not all pass away, but we all will be transformed —

in a single instant, in a single eyelid-blink, when the trumpet sounds for the last time. Because the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised up in non-decayability, and we ourselves will be transformed.
Because this thing that decays must put on non-decayability, and this thing that can die must put on immortality.

When this thing that decays has put on non-decayability, and this thing that can die has put on immortality — in that time, the word that is written will be fulfilled: "Death has been swallowed up in victory."
"O Death, where is your victory? O Death, where is your stinger?"
The stinger of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
But thanks be to God, who (royally) gives victory to us through our (royal) Lord Jesus Christ.

Therefore, my beloved brothers and sisters, be firmly established, unshakable, and have full participation in the work of the (royal) Lord always — knowing that your toil in the (royal) Lord is not in vain.
